purpose: Inventory of deleted and archived governance files identified from git history. Evidence-based classification of each file's status and recommended action.
recovery_source: git history commit 13431e8 (Add files via upload) vs 0f2c238 (Clean up confirmed duplicate auth docs)

entries.analysis: This file contained schema_version 1.1.0 with 81 terms. The current canonical glossary Y_module governance glossary.json is schema_version 1.1.1 and contains all 81 identical terms plus the same authority_hierarchy, conflict_resolution_policy, and glossary_change_policy sections. Zero unique terms exist only in the deleted file. The deleted file was a prior version superseded by Y_module governance glossary.json.
entries.disposition: All content preserved in EAFIX_auth_docs/Y_module governance glossary.json (v1.1.1)
entries.evidence: Term comparison: deleted 81 terms, current 81 terms, intersection 81, unique-to-deleted 0

entries.analysis: This file contained schema_version 1.0.0 of the glossary — the initial seed version with only 4 terms (module_id, canonical_symbol, canonical, module_change_request). It is the earliest ancestor of the current Y_module governance glossary.json (v1.1.1). The 4 seed terms are fully covered in the current glossary. This was a working draft that preceded the bulk terms merge.
entries.disposition: All 4 seed terms preserved in EAFIX_auth_docs/Y_module governance glossary.json
entries.evidence: schema_version 1.0.0 precedes current 1.1.1; all terms are strict subset of current canonical glossary

entries.analysis: This file contained a comprehensive governance design document covering the versioned module system concept. It defined 15 change types (ADD_MODULE, SPLIT_MODULE, MERGE_MODULES, etc.), 9 lifecycle statuses (proposed through superseded), the domain prefix table (F, D, C, S, R, O, B, E, P, U, SK, EA, GOV), ordinal stability rules, file naming conventions, and contract naming rules. This is the primary source for module_lifecycle_governance.json and module_naming_governance.json. The content is NOT currently represented in any registered authority file.
entries.disposition: Governance rules extracted into module_lifecycle_governance.json and module_naming_governance.json
entries.evidence: grep search of all JSON files in EAFIX_auth_docs confirms no existing file captures the change_type enum, lifecycle status table, prefix table, or ordinal rules

entries.analysis: This file was a copy of the canonical routing instructions file. The canonical version exists at the repository root as eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json. The EAFIX_auth_docs copy was a duplicate that could create competing routing authority — its deletion was correct per Principle 1 (no parallel authority systems).
entries.disposition: Canonical version exists at repository root: eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json
entries.evidence: Repository memory: canonical routing file is eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json at repository root

additional_files_analyzed.analysis: 72 terms across 8 categories. All terms are a strict subset of the 81 terms in Y_module governance glossary.json. This is the pre-merge working file referenced in the Schema_module_governance_glossary.txt commit history. Per the U_module governance glossary.txt commit note: 'module governance glossary bulk terms.json (pre-merge working artifact, not an authority source)'.
additional_files_analyzed.disposition: Content preserved in Y_module governance glossary.json; file retained as historical working artifact

additional_files_analyzed.analysis: 77 terms plus authority_hierarchy, conflict_resolution_policy, glossary_change_policy, and validator_targets. All 77 terms are a strict subset of the 81 terms in Y_module governance glossary.json. This appears to be an intermediate working version between the bulk terms and the final v1.1.0 glossary.
additional_files_analyzed.disposition: Content preserved in Y_module governance glossary.json; file retained as historical working artifact

additional_files_analyzed.analysis: Contains a git-format patch file documenting the transition from bulk terms to the canonical glossary. Serves as a migration record rather than an active governance document.

governance_gaps_identified: No module_lifecycle_governance.json existed — rules from Mod_refactor_plan.txt were not in any registered authority file
No module_naming_governance.json existed — naming conventions from Mod_refactor_plan.txt were not in any registered authority file
No identity_crosswalk.json existed — alias history was partially captured in identifier_map.json description but not as a structured crosswalk
doc_authority.json was missing entries for the glossary, lifecycle governance, naming governance, and identity crosswalk files
